descriptionParagraphs: The Hypto Krypto is a balance of tradition and modern performance. Suited to the elite level surfer to the everyday surfer, it is often referred to as the 'one board quiver' for its versatility across all types of surf conditions -- from small 1-3 ft beach breaks, to barrels of up to 8ft.

Skill level: The Hypto Krypto is suitable for all skill levels. For those who are experienced, surf it 1 - 2 inches shorter than your everyday shortboard. For those starting out order it 2 inches bigger than your everyday shortboard. If you're stepping down from a longboard or mini mal, order 6'2 and up.

Conditions: As it's an everyday model it's designed to catch more waves in a range of conditions. It will easily generate speed down the line at your local beach break and will hold up when the conditions present themselves.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks. The Hypto Krypto will work at any location.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 5
FCSII 5

Technology: FutureFlex

Fin system: Futures 3
FCSII 3

The Holy Hypto is a combination of the Hypto Krypto and Holy Grail models, thus the Holy Hypto was created. Having more entry rocker, longer rail line with a performance nose, this shape will surf like your everyday driver with the speed and flow of the Hypto.

The wide point is forward of center allowing a lot of hidden foam to be under the front foot giving the surfer a larger sweet spot while surfing and great paddle power. Ridden 2" longer than your Hypto Krypto, it will suit a great variety of waves with a high performance approach.
Skill level: The Holy Hypto is suitable for those progressing in surfing ability to advanced surfers looking for the performance to match everyday waves. Ridden 2" longer than your Hypto Krypto
Conditions: It is an everyday driver designed to work in waist high to 8ft face waves.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks the Holy Hypto will perform in all round conditions.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 5
FCSII 5

descriptionParagraphs: The Hypto Krypto Twin is a performance keel fin design, with versatility and forgiveness allowing ease of transition from your other boards to this shape. It features a slightly tighter swallow tail and fins set further back to set the tail into the wave and bring control and smooth rail to rail transition. It feels and rides very similar to the Hypto Krypto with the wide point set forward, high volume up under the chest, and concaves that are smooth and fast to surf on. It is a great all round keel fin twin design that will feel comfortable under your feet in all types of waves.
Skill level: The Hypto Krypto Twin is suitable for intermediate to experienced surfers. Ride it the same dimensions as your Hypto Krypto or 4-5" less than your shortboard with 1-3L extra volume.
Conditions: With great paddle power, and plenty of speed, you will be able to draw lines and flow in all types of waves up to 8ft faces.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks where you want to surf around the face with speed.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures Twin
FCSII Twin

descriptionParagraphs: The Step Up everyone has been waiting for. Ride it 1-2" longer than your Hypto Krypto. The Step Up is designed to take the Hypto Krypto surfer on a new journey. Surf it when the waves are more critical.

Skill level: The Hypto Krypto Step Up is designed to surf when the conditions present themselves. You'll get in nice and early with plenty of hold when the conditions are above head high. This is not a model to surf in weak conditions under head high. Intermediate to advanced surfers.
Conditions: As the name suggests, this is a step up model. Designed to surf in good conditions at any location. When your local break is on this is your go to.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks when the conditions present themselves.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 5
FCSII 5

descriptionParagraphs:
The Holy Hypto is a combination of the Hypto Krypto and Holy Grail models, thus the Holy Hypto was created. Having more entry rocker, longer rail line with a performance nose, this shape will surf like your everyday driver with the speed and flow of the Hypto.

The wide point is forward of center allowing a lot of hidden foam to be under the front foot giving the surfer a larger sweet spot while surfing and great paddle power. Ridden 2" longer than your Hypto Krypto, it will suit a great variety of waves with a high performance approach.

Noel Salas - Surf'n'Show - "This board is by far, the best high performance, top to bottom surfing I've experienced. I could not believe how fast this board was getting top to bottom, with razor sharp turns. This board is absolutely mental. It shocked me!"

Skill level: ALL LEVELS
The Holy Hypto is suitable for those progressing in surfing ability to advanced surfers looking for the performance to match everyday waves. Ridden 2" longer than your Hypto Krypto

Conditions: ALL CONDITIONS
It is an everyday driver designed to work in waist high to 8ft face waves.

Wave faces: WAIST TO HEAD AND HALF HIGH
Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks the Holy Hypto will perform in all round conditions.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU
Fin system: Futures 3

Modelled after our iconic Hypto Krypto, the Hypto Krypto Soft Top is a balance of tradition and modern performance with an added boost of fun. Built with a Full Grip Textured Deck, and shaped rails based on the original Hypto, this superior soft top offers performance capabilities you'd never expect from previous soft tops. Suited for any caliber of surfer---everyday to elite---the Hypto Softie's versatility and likeness to the original allows you to excel in surf even beyond small beach breaks. At the very least: an extremely durable shape you're sure to have a blast on.

Deck-3mm PE closed cell cushioned foam with textured traction.

Bottom-Durable Thermoformed ASA bottom skin-UV and heat resistant.

Core-EPS core covered with two layers of glass on deck and single bottom.
Stringer-Carbon T-Stringer for overall strength and durability.
Skill level: Suited for any caliber of surfer---everyday to elite.
Conditions: Beyond small beach breaks.
Technology: EPS
Fin system: Futures 3

The Haydenshapes Atelier Performance Cruiser is our original Cruiser's cousin that rips. A perfect full volumed, stable shortboard crafted for all-around surfing, the Performance Cruiser is made of traditional PU polyester materials and features a single concave throughout the board for maximum speed and lift. With a balanced profile to bring enough forward volume, this incredibly fun shape ensures you can still find the performance and sensitivity within the design on every wave, for all skill level of surfer.

Skill level: The Performance Cruiser is crafted for beginner to intermediate level surfers, but leans more toward the latter—or beginners that are leveling up and really starting to perform. Our smaller dims and volume options in the Performance Cruiser illustrate this.

Conditions: The Performance Cruiser is a solid, user-friendly shortboard model designed to catch all types of waves. Its single concave and balanced profile can also work perfectly for surfers in more advanced waves and conditions.
Wave faces: From grovelly beach breaks to punchier reefs and point breaks, the Performance Cruiser excels in a variety of wave sizes.
Technology: PU
Fin system: Futures 3

The Haydenshapes Atelier Cruiser is where user-friendly meets fun and has a 4-hour-long surf together. A full volumed, 2+1 design made of traditional PU polyester materials, the Cruiser is tailormade for the beginner to intermediate level of surfer and brings both flow and stability to your game. With ample volume the Cruiser paddles like a dream and its wider nose planshape makes it easy to find your feet on the wave and, well---cruise. Its pin tail design also gives you great hold when gliding or drawing flowing lines on the open wave face.

Skill level: The Cruiser is handcrafted for beginner to intermediate level surfers, which is precisely why we've upped the volume and overall dims in order to make them user-friendly. If you lean more toward the beginner level, go with the higher-volume shape---you'll catch waves easier.

Conditions: The Cruiser is an everyday model designed to catch a variety of waves in non-threatening conditions. The full-volumed design easily catches waves and generates speed down the line at your local beach break---or at any other wave where you'd like to cruise.

Wave faces: From soft beach breaks to user-friendly reefs and points, the Cruiser's wider nose planshape helps you to glide through sections and draw stylish lines at any location where it's ideally less than overhead-high.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU
Fin system: Futures 2+1

The Haydenshapes Atelier Retro Longboard provides a nice stable platform for surfers of all levels to find their groove. The outline drips with tradition and features classic 50/50 rails designed to hold you in the wave face as you take a stroll to the nose. When combined with our single concave entry it generates plenty of lift to keep you there all day as well, the contours continue to flow through to flat and finally soft vee out the tail with the classic single fin to capture the traditional feeling of the old school longboard. Paddling is a breeze with the full plan shape to have you catching anything that comes your way.
Skill level: for surfers of all levels

Technology: FutureFlex
PU
Fin system: Futures

The New Wave Mid is a versatile mid length to suit any conditions. It has refined neutral rails with a rolled entry flowing into a slight double vee out the tail.

This shape came from many requests to create an an all round Mid Length model to surf in a wide variety of waves with an entry rocker that will suit the pocket and allow smooth rail to rail surfing.

Skill level: Intermediate to Advanced

Addictive in nature, the speed and lines you can draw will keep you entertained in 1 to 6ft conditions. Suitable for all skill levels.

Conditions: ALL CONDITIONS

Suit to everyday beach breaks and points.

Wave faces: FLAT TO HEAD HIGH

Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks.

descriptionParagraphs: The Nova is a high-speed, small wave driver built for everyday conditions - easy to paddle, fast off the mark, and effortlessly responsive underfoot. Its wider outline carries momentum through flat sections while the refined rocker and bottom contours keep it lively, predictable and composed in the pocket. Designed for wave count, flow and that electric HS feel, the Nova delivers reliable performance for all surfers wanting a board that lights up every session without needing perfect surf.
Skill level: Suited to all surfers from beginner to advanced, recommended to be ridden 2-4 inches shorter than your standard shortboard or ridden at your preferred target volume.

Conditions: The NOVA was created to excel in all conditions from fast punchy beach breaks to smooth, clean wave faces and even the worlds manmade wave pools. It has enough surface area through the nose and tail to generate speed through flatter sections of wave coupled with a medium rocker which will give a responsive feel when surfed tight in the pocket in steeper conditions.
Wave faces: Suitable for wave faces from flat to overhead, the NOVA adapts effortlessly to beach breaks, reefs, points, and especially pools.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 5
FCSII 5

descriptionParagraphs: Fast, fierce and responsive. The UNTITLED is back, fresh faced and tweaked to team spec. "Untitled" is a lively board that defines speed, versatility and fun. Having a slightly fuller plan shape, this winged swallow tail shortboard has a deep single concave to vee that gets you up to top-speed in the mushiest of conditions.
Skill level: The Untitled V2 is designed for the progressive surfer looking to improve their surfing or for the professional looking for a small wave performance shortboard.

Conditions: It is a board that can ride a flatter less powerful wave with plenty of paddle, speed and performance, although it will still have plenty of controlled response in a more curved section of a wave.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks. The Untitled V2 will perform

Fast, fierce and responsive. The UNTITLED is back, fresh faced and tweaked to team spec. "Untitled" is a lively board that defines speed, versatility and fun. Having a slightly fuller plan shape, this winged swallow tail shortboard has a deep single concave to vee that gets you up to top-speed in the mushiest of conditions.
Skill level: The Untitled V2 is designed for the progressive surfer looking to improve their surfing or for the professional looking for a small wave performance shortboard.

descriptionParagraphs: We wanted to design a twin to progress your surfing; not to dumb it down. The MISC. is an extraordinary twin (or 2+1) that will actually ride like your performance board --- top to bottom --- but you'll be flying around the wave with that drag-free twin fin feeling. Astoundingly drivey, super-quick and loose above the lip when searching for release, the MISC. is a team-rider favourite, now available for those wanting that elusive performance twin.

Skill level: Intermediate to Advanced. The MISC. is a performance 2+1 model that benefits from the speed of a twin but holds like a thruster when you surf it top to bottom. For surfers who are looking to spice up their quiver and know how to navigate sections to maximise a waves potential.
Conditions: The MISC. will perform in nearly all conditions. The flat rocker will plane through surface chop and you'll be making sections you never thought were possible.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks. The MISC. will perform in anything under head high.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: FCSII 2+1

descriptionParagraphs: The Holy Grail is a model that sits a little above the Hypto Krypto in terms of ability to perform tight critical moves in the pocket. It is a board that your average surfer can ride and catch a lot of waves, but experienced surfers can also push this board into some very high performance waves and manoeuvres.
Skill level: The Holy Grail is suited to surfers who can comfortably pop up on every wave, travel down the line and are working on progressing their turns. This is not a beginner model.

Conditions: As it's an everyday model the volume under your chest gives great paddling power, allowing you to catch a lot of waves. The Holy Grail will shine in all conditions, even on shore slop, so long as there is a bit of punch in the wave to activate the side cuts throughout the tail.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks. The Holy Grail will work at any location. It loves to be surfed in the pocket.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 5

descriptionParagraphs: The Psychedelic Germ is a highly responsive, shorter, wider surfboard with added features that make it ideal for innovative surfing. The design concept of The Shred Sled model was used as the starting premise in the creation of the Psychedelic Germ. One of the key notable changes was the addition of displacement hull designed by both Hayden Cox and well-known Australian Naval Architect, Andy Dovell. The second key feature being the snowboard inspired side cuts. Similar to that of a boat design, the hull softens the water flow through the entry of the board. It hits the ridge of the hull, and is dispersed into the single concave. When taking off late, this assists in planing through choppy water or helping to land critical manoeuvres. The side cuts shorten the radius of turns, meaning the board will draw a much tighter arc. During bottom turns, it engages the 3 dimensional curve that the side cut creates, which draws the surfer up the wave face. This then transitions onto the inside rail which pulls the surfer around and back into the pocket with ease. For a shorter and wider board that's strikes the balance between performance and innovation, the Psychedelic Germ has all the features needed to bring a whole new dimension to surfing.
Skill level: Intermediate to Advanced
Conditions: Everyday/ All conditions
Wave faces: 2-6ft Wave Faces

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 3
FCSII 3

Wave faces: Waist to Head and Half High (Clean/Rippable to Pumping/Hollow)

descriptionParagraphs:
The Virtue is a refined small-wave hybrid combining the proven flow of the Hypto Krypto with the agility of the Psychedelic Germ. Designed for playful 1-3ft conditions, it thrives in rip bowls, beach breaks, points and wave pools. It delivers effortless speed, smooth rail-to-rail transition and a tighter, more responsive feel in the pocket.

Built to paddle easily and surf intuitively, The Virtue generates speed naturally in softer waves while still allowing controlled release when you want to push harder. It's designed to be ridden 1-2" shorter than your regular Hypto Krypto to unlock its lively, performance-driven character.

Skill level: SKILL LEVEL -- 2-10
Suitable for a wide range of surfers. Experienced riders can size 1-2" shorter than their everyday shortboard for maximum responsiveness, while those wanting extra stability can size closer to their standard shortboard dims.
Conditions: Designed for everyday 1-3ft surf. It excels in beach breaks, rip bowls, playful points and wave pools where speed and quick direction changes matter.

Wave faces: FLAT TO HEAD HIGH
Best suited to small-to-medium wave faces that reward flow, agility and fast response.

Technology: FutureFlex
PU

Fin system: Futures 3
FCSII 3

descriptionParagraphs: A performance short board model designed to surf good waves with maximum response. This model was created on the spot (with a lot of thought) for the 2016 Stab In The Dark and due to the feedback from independent reviews and our team, we finally gave it a spot in the model line up. It is a board that will like a curvey high performance wave that will allow you to surf around the pocket. It will draw longer lines off the front foot rail line, but then will aggressively react and turn tight off the back foot.

descriptionParagraphs: The Cohort II MiG edition is a Micky Clarke signature model, high performance everyday driver to grovel those head high and below days. The speed, responsiveness and release will allow you to make the most of these waves. Ridden 1" shorter than the Cohort I model, this board carries more surface area in the plan shape and has a shallow swallow tail which allows for pivot in the pocket and release in the lip.
Skill level: The Cohort II is suitable for those intermediate to advanced surfers looking for the performance to match everyday waves. Ridden 1" shorter than the Cohort I model.
Conditions: It is an everyday driver designed to grovel all conditions up to head high.
Wave faces: Anything from beach breaks, reef to point breaks the Cohort II will perform in all round conditions.
Technology: FutureFlex

Fin system: Futures 3
FCSII 3

The Cohort I is the staple shortboard in the lineup, which rides fast and free. Building upon a rockerline used in many of the HS shortboards it allows this board to keep effortless and fast flow between turns.

Skill level: A balanced volume distribution throughout puts performance and sensitivity at the forefront of the models design whilst still allowing for easy paddling. A perfect all rounder shortboard for all skill levels.
Conditions: The Cohort I is designed around a user friendly outline that suits a range of conditions. Making it the perfect everyday soldier in your quiver.
Wave faces: All locations and conditions when the waves offer a few sections to work with. Not suitable below waist high.
